title: dysoxylum parasiticum
intro: Dysoxylum parasiticum, also known as the "Mountain Mahogany" or "Tawa", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Dysoxylum parasiticum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to relieve congestion. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including sore throats, bronchitis, and skin infections.
